title: Go Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Go source files contain code written in the Go programming language (also called Golang), developed by Google. Go is a statically typed, compiled language designed for simplicity, fast build times, and built-in concurrency via goroutines and channels.

## When to use
Use [Go Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:go;textable;code) for any `.go` file, including application code, test files (which follow the `_test.go` naming convention), and generated code. Go projects are organized into modules declared by `go.mod`.

## Notes
Go source is UTF-8 text. The `gofmt` tool enforces a canonical formatting style; unformatted Go code is considered non-idiomatic. Go supports build constraints (formerly `// +build`, now `//go:build`) embedded in source comments, which implementers must parse to understand conditional compilation.
